title: Tārā (Ugra / Bhuvaṇeśvarī) Sādhana
subcategory: Mahāvidyā (second) — Ugra Tārā / Ekajaṭā / Nīlasarasvatī (Hindu Śākta)
tradition: Śākta-Tantra (Mahāvidyā / Dasa-Mahāvidyā); Hindu distinct from Tibetan Buddhist Tārā
century: Medieval (c. 8th–12th c.); digest literature c. 16th–19th c.
description: The second of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, the Hindu Tārā ("she who ferries across") is Ugra-Tārā — dark-blue or black-complexioned, standing on a corpse (*śava*), adorned with a garland of severed heads, holding a chopper and a skull-cup. Distinct from the Buddhist Green Tārā / White Tārā. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite of the Śākta cremation-ground tradition. Preserved here as heritage / sour
